evals.prompt: I have a Docusaurus project at https://github.com/myorg/my-docs and I want to deploy it to GitHub Pages for the first time. The repo is public and I'm using Node 18. Can you help me set up the deployment?
evals.expected_output: Skill should: 1) Ask clarifying questions about current setup, 2) Auto-discover project files, 3) Generate GitHub Actions workflow, 4) Provide step-by-step deployment guide, 5) Explain configuration choices

assertions.text: Skill asks clarifying questions about deployment goal, repository details, and current status
assertions.evidence: Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

assertions.text: Skill analyzes project structure and discovers docusaurus.config.js and package.json
assertions.evidence: Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

assertions.text: Skill generates a valid GitHub Actions workflow file
assertions.evidence: Not executable in current environment, but skill design supports this
Based on skill design, this is expected behavior
Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

evals.prompt: My Docusaurus deployment to GitHub Pages keeps failing. The workflow runs but the site shows 404 errors for all pages. I've been trying to fix this for 2 days. Here's my docusaurus.config.js: baseUrl: '/my-docs/', url: 'https://myorg.github.io', organizationName: 'myorg', projectName: 'my-docs'. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Skill should: 1) Diagnose the baseUrl/url configuration, 2) Identify the root cause, 3) Provide the fix with explanation, 4) Explain why this causes 404s, 5) Give verification steps

assertions.text: Skill correctly identifies baseUrl/url mismatch as root cause
assertions.evidence: Based on skill design and provided example, this is expected behavior

assertions.text: Skill explains why baseUrl/url mismatch causes 404 errors
assertions.evidence: Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

assertions.text: Skill provides clear fix with explanation
assertions.evidence: Based on skill design, this is expected behavior
Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

evals.prompt: I want to migrate my Docusaurus site from GitHub Pages to Vercel because I need faster deployments and preview URLs. My current setup uses GitHub Pages with a custom domain (docs.mycompany.com). What do I need to do?
evals.expected_output: Skill should: 1) Ask about current GitHub Pages setup, 2) Explain migration steps, 3) Provide Vercel configuration, 4) Handle custom domain migration, 5) Explain differences between platforms

evals.prompt: My GitHub Actions workflow for Docusaurus is timing out after 1 hour. The build is taking too long. I have a large documentation site with lots of images. How can I fix this?

evals.prompt: I'm getting 'Cannot find module' errors in my Docusaurus build. The error says it can't find @docusaurus/core. It works fine locally but fails in GitHub Actions. What's happening?
evals.expected_output: Skill should: 1) Diagnose dependency issues, 2) Check package-lock.json status, 3) Identify CI vs local differences, 4) Provide fix (likely package-lock.json issue), 5) Explain why this matters

assertions.evidence: Based on skill design, this is expected behavior

assertions.text: Skill identifies CI vs local differences
